Metadata.title: Jingle - desktop command center
Metadata.description: A desktop command center for extensions, AI workflows, and local knowledge.

Site.description: A desktop command center for extensions, AI workflows, and local knowledge.

Roadmap.title: Jingle desktop platform roadmap
Roadmap.body: Jingle is building a desktop command platform for individuals and teams. This roadmap outlines the staged direction across command entry, extensions, AI, connected accounts, team collaboration, and platform governance.
Roadmap.statusBody: Roadmap phases describe product progress. Preview capabilities are ready for early extension development and private integrations, in-development areas are actively being built, and planned areas represent committed direction while experience and timing continue to evolve. Release timing is communicated through official release notes.

sections.title: Launcher, search, and actions
sections.body: App launch, command search, action panels, hotkeys, recent usage, and result ranking are the base surface.
sections.next: Next: file search, cross-source actions, aliases, hotkey management, and more stable ranking.

sections.title: Files, snippets, and clipboard
sections.body: File search, file actions, text snippets, variable expansion, clipboard history, and quick notes will become the local productivity layer.
sections.next: Planned direction: privacy filters, local indexing, snippet variables, pinned history, search, floating notes, and sync strategy.

sections.title: Extensions and system commands
sections.body: Extension packages, command views, menu bar entries, no-view commands, and system capabilities form the extensible desktop layer.
sections.next: Next: scaffolding, package checks, permission display, window management, script commands, calculator, emoji, translator, calendar, Focus, and other frequent local tools.

sections.title: AI commands and agent tools
sections.body: AI should read user-approved context and call extension tools inside visible approval boundaries.
sections.next: Next: Quick AI, AI Command templates, context selection, AI profile, schema-form argument editing, failure recovery, result explanation, and evals.

sections.title: Store, publishing, and updates
sections.body: The extension ecosystem needs a public directory, private distribution, review, signing, updates, withdrawal, and install experience.
sections.next: In progress: publishing flow, review queue, package signing, version compatibility, update policy, and user-readable permission descriptions.

sections.title: Connected accounts and OAuth
sections.body: Public providers such as GitHub and Notion should use platform-owned OAuth, callbacks, refresh, revoke, and secure storage.
sections.next: In progress: provider registry, Connect UI, state validation, token exchange, refresh, revoke, and connection diagnostics.

sections.title: Team sharing and sync
sections.body: The team edition will cover shared quicklinks, snippets, private extensions, organization members, personal settings, and desktop sync.
sections.next: Planned direction: organization permissions, audit, conflict handling, import/export, theme/profile sync, disable-sync controls, and local-first deletion semantics.

sections.title: Security, privacy, and governance
sections.body: Credential boundaries, permission display, extension review, redacted logs, and compatibility policy decide whether the platform can open up long term.
sections.next: Ongoing work: permission model, review rules, redacted error reporting, compatibility matrix, and user-inspectable surfaces.

notes: Jingle is focused on desktop workflows. Mobile apps are outside the current roadmap.
Account credentials are managed by the platform security boundary; extensions do not directly hold OAuth tokens or refresh tokens.
Sync follows local-first and explicit opt-in principles. Local ownership remains the default boundary.

Brand.title: F-05H is the official Jingle brand direction.
Brand.body: The final mark is the command capsule looking right, ready to act. It is not a mascot portrait; it is a desktop command entry with a little life while staying firmly a tool.

final.title: A command companion ready to move.
final.body: The dark rounded tile carries desktop app stability. The light capsule means command input and search. The green status dot means ready. The upper-right signal arc makes the mark feel responsive rather than static.

items.title: Command entry
items.body: It should first read as a place to search, input, and run.

items.title: Ready to act
items.body: The gaze moves right without becoming an exaggerated face.

items.title: Desktop feel
items.body: The mark is tuned for Dock, menu bar, website, and developer docs.

assets.title: One mark works across real product states.
assets.body: The official set covers desktop app icon, web logo, favicon, small sizes, light and dark use, flat, mono, full lockup, apparel, and merchandise previews.

cards.title: Desktop app icon
cards.body: Readable in the Dock, with a dark green tile and light command capsule.

cards.title: Web logo
cards.body: Pairs with the Jingle wordmark in navigation and docs while staying product-like.

cards.title: Favicon
cards.body: Small sizes keep the capsule, search read, and green dot while reducing detail.

cards.title: Light / dark
cards.body: The main icon does not split into two identities; its own tile works on both backgrounds.

cards.title: Flat mark
cards.body: For diagrams, compact UI, and surfaces that need lower rendering complexity.

cards.title: Monochrome mark
cards.body: For print, embossing, partner placements, and minimal UI contexts.

cards.title: Full lockup
cards.body: For launch graphics, brand pages, partner pages, and full brand presence.

cards.title: Apparel
cards.body: Works best as a small badge rather than an oversized decorative graphic.

cards.title: Merch
cards.body: Fits stickers, mugs, tote bags, and lightweight team material.

tokens.title: Website tokens and brand assets stay from the same source.
tokens.body: The website background, panels, accents, focus ring, icon shadow, and brand previews use the same color and radius system so pages do not drift apart.

downloads.title: Official asset list
downloads.body: Use SVG for design and web surfaces. Use PNG for app icons, favicons, small previews, documentation screenshots, and external material.

hero.title: A faster way to work with your desktop.
hero.subtitle: Jingle is a command center for launching workflows, connecting extensions, and giving AI the context it needs to help without taking over your workspace.
hero.searchPlaceholder: Search apps, commands, files, and agents

demoCards.title: Extension actions
demoCards.body: Run connected workflows from one surface instead of switching apps.

demoCards.title: AI with context
demoCards.body: Attach files, memories, and tools while keeping approval visible.

features.title: Everything starts from the command surface.
features.body: Built for repeated work: quick to invoke, easy to scan, and explicit about what runs.

pillars.title: Command center
pillars.description: Launch commands, switch context, and operate repeat workflows without leaving the keyboard.

pillars.title: Extension store
pillars.description: Connect apps, run extension views, and bring provider actions into one predictable surface.

pillars.title: AI workspace
pillars.description: Give AI workflows the right files, tools, memories, and approval boundaries.

pillars.title: Local knowledge
pillars.description: Keep working context local-first, searchable, editable, and visible to the user.

workflow.title: Find, act, and hand off in one place.

rows.title: Find anything
rows.detail: Search, open, and act across apps.

rows.title: Do the work
rows.detail: Run extension commands with native-feeling views.

rows.title: Hand off safely
rows.detail: Let agents use approved tools and local context.

extensions.title: Connect the tools your work already depends on.

cards.title: GitHub
cards.description: Issues, pull requests, notifications, and repository workflows.

cards.title: Notion
cards.description: Pages, databases, capture flows, and workspace search.

cards.title: Reminders
cards.description: Native reminders, due dates, quick capture, and local task flows.

roadmap.title: Jingle's desktop platform roadmap.
roadmap.body: Jingle is building a desktop workflow platform around launcher commands, extensions, AI tools, team distribution, and cross-device sync. The roadmap presents product progress and future direction by stage.

items.title: Launcher and file search
items.description: App launch, command search, file search, action panels, aliases, and hotkeys are the base of the desktop surface.

items.title: Extension ecosystem and store
items.description: Extension packages, runtime SDK, developer tooling, review, publishing, and public or private distribution need one complete loop.

items.title: AI commands and tool calls
items.description: AI commands call extension tools, explain inputs, show approvals, and leave inspectable results.

items.title: Team sharing
items.description: Shared quicklinks, snippets, private extensions, member permissions, and organization settings will move as part of the team edition.

items.title: Windows, clipboard, and system commands
items.description: Window management, clipboard history, snippet expansion, system commands, calculator, and emoji need native-level ergonomics.

items.title: Connected accounts and OAuth
items.description: Public integrations need platform-owned OAuth, callbacks, token refresh, revoke, and a standard Connect UI.

items.title: Migration, import, and sync
items.description: Settings, extensions, quicklinks, snippets, history, and local knowledge need import, backup, and sync paths.

items.title: Security, privacy, and governance
items.description: Credential boundaries, permission display, extension review, redacted reporting, and compatibility policy decide whether the platform can open up long term.

items.title: Credential boundaries
items.description: Sensitive credentials belong to host-managed secure storage, not plain extension settings.

items.title: User control
items.description: Users should understand which context is local, which is shared, and what agents can access.

items.title: Inspectable state
items.description: Connections, memories, and tool permissions are designed as inspectable product surfaces.

cta.title: Explore the Jingle product roadmap.
cta.body: See desktop platform capabilities, upcoming direction, and developer resources.

Docs.title: Build commands, extensions, connected accounts, and agent tools.
Docs.body: Technical guides for building on Jingle, from extension packages to account connections and agent tool boundaries.

cards.title: Extension model
cards.description: Build view commands, no-view commands, menu bar commands, and AI tools.

cards.title: Connections
cards.description: Account connection flow, secure storage, and connection state.

cards.title: Agent tools
cards.description: How agents request tool access, present approvals, and report results.

Connections.title: Connected accounts are part of the platform contract.
Connections.body: Extensions declare the accounts and scopes they need. Jingle owns the browser handoff, validation, secure storage, refresh, revoke, and connection state.

providers.title: GitHub
providers.body: Use OAuth App or GitHub App depending on permission shape. GitHub App gives finer permissions and short-lived installation or user credentials.

providers.title: Notion
providers.body: Public connections use OAuth. Users approve workspace and page access, then Jingle stores the approved account connection for extension commands.

requirements: Provider registry for GitHub, Notion, and future integrations.
Authorization code flow with state validation and PKCE where supported.
Desktop return route through custom URL scheme or loopback redirect.
Secure credential storage separate from plain extension preferences.
Connection states: connected, missing, expired, revoked, scope-missing.
Standard Connect UI for commands that require account access.

steps.title: Declare
steps.description: Extensions declare provider, scopes, return target, and credential requirements.

steps.title: Authorize
steps.description: Jingle opens the provider page with state and PKCE where the provider supports it.

steps.title: Store
steps.description: The desktop app finishes the exchange and stores credentials outside plain preferences.

steps.title: Resolve
steps.description: Commands receive connected, missing, expired, or scope-missing state.

ExtensionsDocs.title: A package model for desktop commands and agent tools.
ExtensionsDocs.body: Jingle extensions should feel native: fast command views, clear settings, account connections, and AI tools that can be inspected before they act.

layers.title: Manifest
layers.body: Manifest owns title, icon, commands, runtime modes, preferences, and platform support.

layers.title: Runtime
layers.body: Runtime package owns React command views, no-view commands, menu bar surfaces, and SDK calls.

layers.title: Main
layers.body: Main package owns privileged host calls, local APIs, filesystem bridges, and native integrations.

layers.title: Agent tools
layers.body: AI package exposes typed tools with permission metadata and human-readable presentation.

Privacy.title: Jingle is designed around local ownership and explicit connections.
Privacy.body: Our privacy model starts with local control: users should know what is stored, what is connected, and what an agent or extension can access.

sections.title: Local-first product data
sections.body: Long-term memory, workspace context, extension preferences, and account connection metadata are stored locally first. Server sync, when introduced, will be explicit and reversible.

sections.title: Sensitive credentials
sections.body: Account credentials belong in secure storage and stay separate from plain settings files, logs, telemetry, and extension-visible preference objects.

sections.title: Extension boundaries
sections.body: Extensions only receive the capabilities and connection state needed for the command they are running. Jingle owns account connection, refresh, revocation, and provider responses.

sections.title: User control
sections.body: Users can inspect connected accounts, revoke access, delete local memories, and distinguish local data from synchronized data.

Callback.title: Finish connecting your account.
Callback.body: This page is used when an external service returns to Jingle after sign-in. If Jingle is open, the desktop app will finish the connection there.
Callback.errorBody: Return to Jingle and try connecting again. If the problem continues, check the connection guide.
Callback.readyBody: You can return to the desktop app. Jingle will complete the connection and take you back to the command that asked for access.
